judgments.reasoning: Both identify the null/undefined crash on mainHostDestinationCalendar when destinationCalendar is null or empty.
No candidate discusses the redundant optional chain after an existing ternary guard.
Both describe a logic-inversion where a find predicate always fails due to comparing a value against itself or its falsy guard; C1 is the best match for the updateEvent path.
Both describe the IS_TEAM_BILLING_ENABLED boolean inversion in org creation, resulting in incorrect slug/requestedSlug assignment.
No candidate addresses the Calendar interface contract mismatch where some implementations omit the credentialId parameter.
Both identify the same unreachable branches in getBaseConditions because the Prisma.Sql object is always truthy.
No candidate addresses the org-member exclusion issue when teamsFromOrg is empty.
Both identify that slotStartTime is incorrectly used instead of slotEndTime for the end-boundary calculation.
Both describe the same bug: === comparing Dayjs objects by reference instead of using .isSame(), causing the override condition to never trigger.
Both identify the same bug where the literal string 'refresh_token' is persisted instead of the actual token value.
Both identify the invalid computed property key in the Zod schema that doesn't work as intended.
Both identify that the safeParse wrapper ({success, data, error}) is being stored/used instead of the actual parsed data.
Both describe the return shape mismatch where a raw fetch Response is used where callers expect a token object with .data.
Same underlying issue as G4: res.data is undefined when the sync endpoint returns a raw Response; C2 identifies this return-shape mismatch.
Both identify the non-atomic read-modify-write on retryCount that loses increments under concurrent access.
Both identify that the delete filter's retryCount > 1 condition lacks a WorkflowMethods.SMS scope, causing non-SMS reminders to be deleted.
Both identify the same case-sensitivity bypass where guest emails aren't lowercased before comparison against the lowercased blacklist.
Both identify the && vs || logic error in the admin/owner permission check.
Both identify that the email notification uses the raw/original guest list instead of the filtered uniqueGuests list.
Both identify that duplicate emails within the input are not deduplicated, leading to duplicate attendee rows.
No candidate addresses the initial state of [''] (array with empty string) in MultiEmail causing validation issues.
Both identify that should_block? performs stat updates (side effects) during what should be a read operation, creating race conditions.
No candidate addresses the regex pattern matching domain suffixes instead of full domains, allowing evil.example.com to match example.com.
Both identify the SSRF vulnerability from calling open(url) without validating the URL/host.
Both identify that indexOf-based origin validation is insufficient as it does substring matching, allowing bypass with a crafted domain.
Both identify that postMessage uses the full referer URL as targetOrigin instead of just the origin, causing messages to be dropped.
Both identify that ALLOWALL is non-standard and disables clickjacking protection; C3 also notes the referer-based bypass.
Both identify XSS from unescaped URL interpolation in generated HTML; C4 matches the XSS part of G5.
Both identify the same invalid 'end if' syntax in the ERB template causing a Ruby SyntaxError.
No candidate addresses the async/unhandled findMembers() call creating a race condition for member data availability.
Both identify an off-by-one issue in pagination where the offset/page calculation produces an empty last page.
No candidate addresses the HTTP method mismatch (PUT vs DELETE) in the remove_member test.
Both identify the thread-safety race condition on @loaded_locales due to reading outside the mutex.
No candidate addresses normalizing the locale input (String vs Symbol) to prevent double-loading.
Both identify that before_validation raises NoMethodError when host is nil (self.host.sub crashes).
C3 and C4 both address the nil dereference when record not found; C3 (destroy path) best matches the core concern in G2 about nil method calls.
Both identify that record_for_host does lower(host) on the DB column but doesn't normalize the incoming URL host, causing case-sensitive mismatches.
No candidate addresses the migration inserting raw SQL without model normalization, causing future host lookups to fail for migrated data with http:// prefixes.
Both identify the check-then-insert race condition where concurrent requests can exceed the device limit.
No candidate addresses that TagDevice returning ErrDeviceLimitReached now blocks anonymous authentication entirely, a behavioral regression from the async approach.
No candidate addresses the compilation error in dbSession.Exec(args...) where the query string is passed as interface{} instead of string.
Both identify that ErrDeviceLimitReached is misleadingly returned when zero rows are updated, even if the device doesn't exist.
No candidate addresses the inconsistency between using device.UpdatedAt and time.Now().UTC() in the time window calculation.
Both identify the missing inner check after acquiring the write lock in the double-checked locking pattern.
No candidate addresses the unconditional assignment of the fetch result to cache even when the fetch fails, caching nil/empty results.
No candidate addresses the implementation issue of picocli.exit() calling System.exit() directly; candidates focus on missing test assertions for exit codes.
No candidates produced for this PR.
No candidates produced for this PR.
No candidates produced for this PR.
Both identify that getSubGroupsCount() returns null in violation of the GroupModel contract, risking NPEs in callers.
Both identify that the reader thread is not joined before the assertion, creating a race condition in the test.
No candidate addresses the issue that sample_rate = 0.0 is falsy and would be skipped in a truthy check.
No candidate addresses the non-deterministic hash() used for cache keys across processes due to hash randomization.
No candidate addresses the upsampling eligibility check using the outer dataset instead of the scoped dataset.
No candidate specifically addresses breaking changes in the error response format.
No candidate addresses the detector validator using the wrong key when updating type.
Both identify that zip(error_ids, events.values()) misaligns data because dict value order isn't guaranteed to match error_ids.
The only candidate addresses a different issue (per-process cap); no candidate covers the inconsistent 'shard'/'shards' metric tagging.
No candidate addresses the fixed sleep in tests causing flakiness.
No candidate addresses the isinstance check always being false for spawn-context processes.
No candidate addresses the monkeypatched time.sleep making the test sleep ineffective.
No candidate addresses the loop break skipping termination of remaining flusher processes.
Both identify the shared mutable default issue where timezone.now() is evaluated at class definition time rather than per-instance.
No candidate addresses the method name typo (inalid vs invalid).
No candidate addresses the method name mismatch ('empty_array' testing an empty dict).
No candidate addresses that to_dict() returning a datetime object may cause JSON serialization failures when passed as task kwargs.
No candidate addresses the potential AttributeError from queue.shutdown(immediate=False) not existing in the standard Python queue module.
No candidate addresses the repeated magic number 50 for max_wait needing extraction as a named constant.
No candidate addresses the docstring mismatch in test_thread_queue_parallel_error_handling.
